cot_prefix: Okay, let's go through this question step by step. The question is asking what the International Olympic Committee (IOC) were concerned about when the jade ring design was proposed. The options are A through D. First, I need to look at the relevant part of the text. The last paragraph says: "Experts from the International Olympic Committee (IOC) were concerned about the risk of fragility when the jade ring design was proposed. The IOC required that jade rings should be strong enough to withstand a 2-meter drop test. The Shanghai Mint raised that standard to 3- meters. Medals can survive a 3-meter drop test with only minor cracks on the jade rings." So here, the key phrase is "risk of fragility." Fragility means something is easily broken or damaged. The IOC was worried that the jade rings might be too fragile, meaning they could break easily. The passage then explains that the IOC set a requirement for the jade rings to withstand a 2-meter drop, which the mint actually increased to 3 meters. Looking at the options: A) the medals will be worth less – The text doesn't mention anything about the monetary value of the medals. It's about structural integrity, not financial value. So probably not A. B) the medals are too difficult to make – The text mentions that it's not easy to bond jade and metal and that the process is painstaking, but the IOC's concern is specifically about the fragility of the jade rings, not the difficulty of making them. So B might not be the right answer. C) competitors don't like the design – There's no mention of competitors' opinions in the text. The concern comes from the IOC experts, not the competitors. So C is likely incorrect. D) jade is fragile and easy to break up – This directly relates to the "risk of fragility" mentioned in the text. The IOC was worried that
